body.u: I left school at the age of sixteen in July 1978 and tried very hard to get a job and eventually I got an apprenticeship at Sheppey Auto Garage, I was very pleased, the apprenticeship lasted five years.

When I had finished there I got a job at a small garage on the island with a man of about 42 dealing in second hand cars and motorcycles and also under goingundergoing major strip down jobs and small repairs, I carryiedcarried on working there until the man who owned died and left all the money and business to me, I worked very hard and eventually had enough money to employ an apprentice myself. I trained him well and then her left, decided to buy myself a grasstrackgrass track bike and start to race it in local meetings on the island, at first I lost every time and once came off and cut my head very badleybadly, however I soon recovered, and found myself winning a few races and taking a few trophies, I carried this on until I was 49 and then gave it up and sold my business, I brought a small house with a large workshop and got myself a brand new motorcycle which I had dreamed of doing ever since I was a kid, I then applied for a part-time job in a local garage there I mainly washed cars and little repairs, at the age of fifteyfifty one I decided to retire, but I still done repairs in my own workshop for people I knew I made quite a bit. I sold my motorcycle and brought myself a Honda 125 just to ride around for pleasure. I never gave up mucking around with motorcycles and cars until I was fifty nine and I was out on my bike and pulled out of a junction without looking and got run down by a large lorry, and I got parralizedparalysed from the waist down and have been in a wheelchair ever since, I regularly go to the local grass track meetings and will continue to until the end of my days.
